senses.definition: morally good, justified, or acceptable
examples: I hope we're doing the right thing
: you were quite right to criticize him

senses.definition: true or correct as a fact
examples: I'm not sure I know the right answer
her theories were proved right

senses.definition: correct in one's opinion or judgment
examples: she was right about Tom having no money

senses.definition: according to what is correct for a particular situation or thing
examples: is this the right way to the cottage?
you're not holding it the right way up

senses.definition: the best or most suitable of a number of possible choices for a particular purpose or occasion
examples: he was clearly the right man for the job
I was waiting for the right moment to ask him

senses.definition: socially fashionable or important
examples: he was seen at all the right places

senses.definition: in a satisfactory, sound, or normal state or condition
examples: that sausage doesn't smell right
if only I could have helped put matters right

senses.definition: on, toward, or relating to the side of a human body or of a thing that is to the east when the person or thing is facing north
examples: my right elbow
the right edge of the field
her right shoe

senses.definition: relating to or denoting a person or group favoring right-wing views; conservative
examples: are you politically right, left, or center?

senses.definition: complete; absolute (used for emphasis, typically in derogatory contexts)
examples: I felt a right idiot

senses.definition: to the furthest or most complete extent or degree (used for emphasis)
examples: the car spun right off the track
I'm right out of ideas

senses.definition: exactly; directly (used to emphasize the precise location or time of something)
examples: Harriet was standing right behind her

senses.definition: immediately; without delaying or hesitating
examples: I'll be right back

senses.definition: very
examples: it's right spooky in there!

senses.definition: correctly
examples: he had guessed right

senses.definition: in the required or necessary way; properly; satisfactorily
examples: nothing's going right for me this season

senses.definition: on or to the right side
examples: turn right at Main Street

senses.definition: that which is morally correct, just, or honorable
examples: she doesn't understand the difference between right and wrong
the rights and wrongs of the matter

senses.definition: a moral or legal entitlement to have or obtain something or to act in a certain way
examples: : she had every right to be angry
you're quite within your rights to ask for your money back
there is no right of appeal against the decision

senses.definition: the authority to perform, publish, film, or televise a particular work, event, etc.
examples: they sold the paperback rights

senses.definition: the right-hand part, side, or direction
examples: take the first turning on the right
: she seated me on her right

senses.definition: the right-hand half of the field when facing the opponent's goal
examples: they made a neat series of passes over on the right

senses.definition: the right wing of an army
examples: the allies succeeded in overrunning the French right

senses.definition: a right turn
examples: he made a right in Dorchester Avenue

senses.definition: a road or entrance on the right
examples: take the first right over the stream

senses.definition: a person's right fist
examples: he ducked down low then brought up his right

senses.definition: a blow given with the right fist
examples: the young cop swung a terrific right

senses.definition: a group or party favoring right-wing or conservative views
examples: the Right got in at the election
his proposal was viewed with alarm by the right of the party

senses.definition: verb [with object] restore to a normal or upright position: we righted the capsized dinghy. • restore to a normal or correct condition or situation: righting the economy demanded major cuts in defense spending. • redress or rectify (a wrong or mistaken action): she was determined to right the wrongs done to her father. • (usually be righted) archaic make reparation to (someone) for a wrong done to them: we'll see you righted.

exclamation informal used to indicate one's agreement or to acknowledge a statement or order: “Barry's here.” “Oh, right” | right you are, sir. • used at the end of a statement to invite agreement, approval, or confirmation: you went to see Angie on Monday, right?. • used as a filler in speech or to introduce an utterance, exhortation, or suggestion: right, let's have a drink | and I didn't think any more of it, right, but Mom said I should take him to a doctor.

notes.content: Old English riht (adjective and noun), rihtan (verb), rihte (adverb), of Germanic origin; related to Latin rectus ‘ruled’, from an Indo-European root denoting movement in a straight line.

items.definition: be morally or legally justified in one's views, actions, or decisions
examples: Sean was not going to apologize as he believed he was in the right

items.definition: if things had happened or been done fairly or correctly
examples: by rights, he should not be playing next week
I want to do right by the child

items.definition: as a result of one's own claims, qualifications, or efforts, rather than an association with someone else
examples: he was already established as a poet in his own right

items.definition: having full control of one's mind; sane or rational.
not having full control of one's mind; not sane or rational.
(of a person) not completely sane or rational.
on the safe, appropriate, or desirable side of
examples: a record that fully embraces the theatricality of its genre but falls just on the right side of ridiculous
he hasn't always remained on the right side of the law
she's on the right side of forty
quite a few of them of them will start playing right away
your record's bad right enough

items.definition: used as an expression of strong support, approval, or encouragement.
with the side intended to be seen or used uppermost
examples: turn the skirt right side out

items.definition: the necessary qualities for a given task or job
examples: he had the right stuff to enter this business

phraseGroups.rawContent: rightable | ˈrīdəbəl | adjective righter | ˈrīdər | noun rightish | ˈrīdiSH | adjective rightless adjective
phraseGroups.title: DERIVATIVES
